README: # ados-catalog

`ados-catalog` is a local, **read-only** GitHub repository cataloging and
semantic repository-QA system. It inventories repositories, extracts ADOS
knowledge into durable append-only JSONL banks (stored in a **separate**
`ados-catalog-banks` repository), builds a semantic index, and answers
natural-language questions with grounded, cited answers.

This repository is the application/CLI/schemas implementation. Generated data
banks live in the companion repo:

| Purpose | Folder | Remote |
|---|---|---|
| App / CLI / schemas / validation | `ados-catalog` | https://github.com/qobeat/ados-catalog |
| Durable JSONL banks, indexes, reports, logs | `ados-catalog-banks` | https://github.com/qobeat/ados-catalog-banks |
payload.title: Repository summary: qobeat/ados-catalog

payload.text_excerpt:
Repository qobeat/ados-catalog-banks.
Owner: qobeat.
Description: n/a.
Topics: n/a.
Primary language: n/a.
Archetype: repository_catalog. Domain: domain:ai_ml. Subdomain: ai_ml/semantic_search.
Rationale: AI/ML signals in archetype, dependencies, or README..
README: # ados-catalog-banks

Generated, append-only JSONL data banks for the ADOS catalog.

- `banks/` append-only ledgers + mutable `restore_point.json`
- `bank-descriptors/` bank descriptors
- `reference-model-banks/` ADOS archetype/domain reference
- `semantic-index/` retrieval store
- `logs/` structured logs
- `reports/` JSON reports

Do not place application source code here. Never commit secrets.
payload.title: Repository summary: qobeat/ados-catalog-banks

payload.text_excerpt:
Repository qobeat/github-catalog.
Owner: qobeat.
Description: To keep track and to summarize repositories.
Topics: n/a.
Primary language: Shell.
Archetype: controlled_spec_or_schema. Domain: domain:ai_ml. Subdomain: ai_ml/semantic_search.
Rationale: AI/ML signals in archetype, dependencies, or README..
README: # **github-catalog**

A minimalistic, zero-dependency (Bash/jq/git) CLI tool for building an append-only catalog of git repositories. It extracts semantic documentation (Goals, Objectives, Requirements) and commit history without cloning full working trees and without using LLMs.  
This project follows strict OSINT and ADLC principles: deterministic execution, verifiable evidence, and strict environment isolation. Read the architecture specification in [docs/ADR-001-github-catalog-rewrite.md](docs/ADR-001-github-catalog-rewrite.md) and the unified CLI design in [docs/ADR-002.md](docs/ADR-002.md).

## **Prerequisites**

* **Bash 5.0+** — required for parallel `wait -n` job control
payload.title: Repository summary: qobeat/github-catalog

README: # chatgpt-extract

**A solo founder's home-lab decision instrument.** It turns your own ChatGPT
export history into three things:

1. **Catalog** — a private, losslessly-extracted, queryable catalog of
   everything you have built (`gpt list/search/show`).
2. **Ask** — semantic recall over that catalog: ask it questions in natural
   language and get answers grounded in *your latest* chats, with citations,
   running entirely on your machine (`gpt index` + `gpt ask`).
3. **Benchmark** — a realistic harness that uses that same real work to decide
   which model, provider, and hardware are actually worth paying for, ending in
   a governed verdict (`gpt metrics/arena` → `AI_MODEL_TESTS.md`).

> The benchmark is grounde
payload.title: Repository summary: qobeat/chatgpt-extract

README: # GitHub Workflows

Active release barrier workflows start in Iteration 6. The docs contract gate is added in Iteration 7. Canonical fixture enforcement is now required across the main release/docs pipelines.

The release barrier is split into:

- `release-barrier.yml` for the Linux blocking suites
- `cross-platform-smoke.yml` for native smoke and Linux musl build smoke
- `docs-command.yml` for the executable docs contract
- `public-release.yml` for tagged public release asset builds and GitHub Release publishing

Required aggregate checks for `v0.1` rele
payload.title: Repository summary: 777genius/lintai

Social Monitor is a monitoring platform for public social, news, and web signals. It combines a TypeScript/NestJS backend, durable worker runtime, generated API contracts, and a web-first Flutter frontend for analyst and operator workflows.

The project is built for teams that need to collect public signals, normalize them into reliable events, search and analyze them, review feed items, gen

ReviewRouter is a GitHub Action for pull request review with Codex, OpenRouter, and experimental CLI providers for Claude Code, Gemini, and OpenCode.

The production-focused installer currently hardens the Codex and OpenRouter paths first. The other provider adapters are still available through `REVIEW_PROVIDERS`, but they are not yet the recommended zero-config setup path.

# Flask Flask is a lightweight [WSGI] web application framework. It is designed to make getting started quick and easy, with the ability to scale up to complex applications. It began as a simple wrapper around [Werkzeug] and [Jinja], and has become one of the most popular Python web application frameworks. Flask offers suggestions, but doesn't enforce any dependencies or project layout. It is up to the developer to choose the tools and libraries they want to use. T
